strengths: 15+ years of full-stack depth with strong TypeScript and Node.js proficiency matching the primary stack
Active AI agent infrastructure work in current role, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Consistent testing culture evidenced by TDD practice, public workshops, and open-source test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
React listed only briefly at one employer (Optilyz); not a demonstrated strength or focus area
Kotlin/Java noted in skills but lacks substantive project depth; PostgreSQL absent
response.justification: The candidate brings substantial full-stack seniority and hands-on TypeScript/Node.js experience that directly matches the primary stack, plus current work on AI agent infrastructure which is a meaningful differentiator. The principal risk is the complete absence of Nest.js — the core backend framework — and thin React exposure, both of which could meaningfully slow ramp-up on existing services. Infrastructure breadth (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes, MongoDB) and a demonstrably strong testing discipline offset some of those gaps. The stack misses are concrete enough to warrant probing in an interview rather than filtering at this stage.
